# Noctrace

> Open-source, local-only Chrome DevTools-style waterfall visualizer for Claude Code agent workflows. Zero configuration. MIT license.

Noctrace reads JSONL session logs from ~/.claude/projects/ and renders them as an interactive timeline showing every tool call, sub-agent spawn, API request, token cost, and context health grade in real time. It runs entirely on localhost with no cloud dependency, no telemetry, and no database. Install with `npx noctrace`.

## What Noctrace does

Noctrace gives Claude Code users a browser-based observability dashboard. It shows tool calls (Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Agent, MCP) as horizontal bars on a shared time axis — identical in concept to Chrome DevTools Network tab. Each row displays: tool type, target file or command, duration, cumulative token count, estimated USD cost, and context fill percentage. Rows turn red when context fill exceeds 80%, making context rot visible before it degrades session quality.

## Key capabilities

- Waterfall timeline: every tool call and agent spawn on a shared time axis with zoom (1–50x) and pan
- Context health scoring: A–F grade computed from context fill (40%), compaction events (25%), file re-reads (15%), error rate acceleration (10%), and tool efficiency trends (10%)
- Sub-agent visibility: collapsible agent groups with nested tool call rows; agent type labels (e.g., "Explore", "core:deep-researcher")
- Token cost estimation: per-row USD cost using Claude public pricing with per-model detection
- Context rot detection: red row tinting, heat strips, compaction boundary markers, and context drift rate (tokens/min growth via 3-window linear regression)
- Efficiency tips: 8 waste patterns flagged with amber lightbulb indicators
- Security tips: 13 security patterns flagged with red shield indicators
- Loop detection: 3+ consecutive identical tool calls trigger a warning
- Advanced filtering: structured syntax — type:bash, >5s, tokens:>1k, error, running
- Session comparison: split-screen health grades, summary metrics, tool mix bars, context fill trajectory sparklines
- Real-time streaming: WebSocket byte-offset incremental parsing; auto-reconnect
- Docker support: `npx noctrace --docker <container>` syncs sessions from running containers
- Devcontainer support: `npx noctrace --devcontainer <path>` resolves the backing Dev Containers container and streams its session logs
- Patterns view: a second top-level tab aggregating across every session in a time window (today / 7 days / 30 days). Panels: health grade distribution with week-over-week regression arrows, project rot leaderboard ranked by D/F session percentage, and tool health grid with per-tool failure rate and p50/p95 latency. No spend or token tracking — surfaces quality and waste
- Multi-agent support: in addition to Claude Code, Noctrace now traces OpenAI Codex CLI sessions from `~/.codex/sessions/`. Same waterfall, same health grade, same real-time streaming. Sub-agent links, per-tool failure detection (exit codes + timeouts), and per-turn token accounting all work. GitHub Copilot Chat tracing is planned for 1.5
- OTel export: sessions exportable as OpenTelemetry traces for Grafana, Jaeger, or Datadog
- MCP session registry: sessions self-register on start; dashboard shows only active sessions

## Installation

npx noctrace

Or install globally:

npm install -g noctrace
noctrace --enable

Requirements: Node.js 20 LTS or later. macOS, Linux, or Windows (WSL). Any modern browser. An existing Claude Code installation.

## How Noctrace works

Noctrace is a single Node.js process. It runs a local Express 5 server on port 4117 that serves a React SPA, exposes a REST API, and upgrades WebSocket connections. The file watcher (chokidar) monitors ~/.claude/projects/ for new or modified JSONL files. When a file changes, the server reads only the new bytes (byte-offset incremental parsing) and pushes the parsed rows to the browser over WebSocket. No data ever leaves the machine.

Session logs are JSONL files written by Claude Code at ~/.claude/projects/<project-slug>/<session-id>.jsonl. Sub-agent logs live at ~/.claude/projects/<project-slug>/<session-id>/subagents/agent-<agent-id>.jsonl. Noctrace parses both formats and links sub-agent rows to their parent agent rows.

## Context rot

Context rot is the progressive degradation of Claude Code session quality as the context window fills. When context fill exceeds 80%, Claude begins losing early instructions, tool outputs, and reasoning chains, which causes repeated work, contradictory edits, and increased error rates. Noctrace makes context rot visible: rows are tinted red above 80% fill, a heat strip runs along the top of the waterfall, compaction boundary lines show where Claude Code triggered automatic context compaction, and a drift rate metric shows how fast the context is growing in tokens per minute.

## Pricing and license

Noctrace is free and open source under the MIT license. Repository: https://github.com/nyktora/noctrace. npm package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/noctrace.
